qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Working plan exception endpoints lack provider ownership verification
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Different root cause: this finding concerns missing provider ownership checks in Calendar.php working plan exception POST endpoints, not the EA_Security.php non-POST CSRF early return or GET-based admin/account state changes described by the advisory.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: API v1 endpoints lack object-level authorization enabling IDOR attacks
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Different root cause: this finding concerns authenticated API v1 object-level authorization failures for attacker-chosen IDs, not browser-driven CSRF through non-POST requests bypassing EA_Security.php token validation.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No manifest-listed qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code or equivalent root cause. The advisory is specifically the CSRF protection bypass caused by EA_Security.php::csrf_verify() returning before token validation for non-POST requests, combined with GET or $_REQUEST state-changing admin/account endpoints. The qualifying findings instead report IDOR/authorization flaws.
evidence: Advisory names application/core/EA_Security.php::csrf_verify() and its non-POST early return as the vulnerable root cause.
Advisory examples are GET-based state-changing endpoints such as index.php/admins/store, index.php/admins/update, and index.php/account/save that bypass CSRF validation.
working_plan_idor reports missing provider ownership verification in application/controllers/Calendar.php save/delete working plan exception POST handlers.
api_idor reports missing object-level authorization in authenticated API v1 endpoints and application/libraries/Api.php authentication behavior.
blockers_to_higher_tier: No advisory-matched qualifying finding was present, so PoC execution was not applicable under the judging rules.
